macro_events.event_summary: Analyst commentary warns that an Iran war could be a $300 billion economic shock, driving up oil prices, mortgage rates, and squeezing real wages.
primary_drivers: Oil supply disruption risk premium (Strait of Hormuz / Middle East crude)
Higher long-end Treasury yields from inflation/risk premia → higher mortgage rates
Defense spending acceleration and geopolitical risk hedging flows

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated supermajor with large upstream leverage to Brent; every $10/bbl move on sustained basis adds meaningful EPS. Direct beneficiary of Middle East supply disruption pricing.
Similar upstream exposure to XOM with additional torque from production growth; benefits directly from oil risk premium.
Pure-play US shale (Permian) with high oil beta; among the most levered large-caps to crude price spikes. No Iranian exposure to lose.
Largest defense prime; missile defense (THAAD, PAC-3) and F-35 demand accelerates with Middle East conflict. Direct revenue tailwind from supplemental DoD appropriations.
Patriot missiles, Tomahawks, and air defense systems are first-line consumables in a Gulf conflict; restock orders flow to RTX directly.
Long-range strike, ISR, and B-21 program benefit from heightened threat environment and supplemental defense budgets.
Texas Pacific Land — royalty stream on Permian production with zero capex; oil price spike flows straight to FCF with no operational risk.

losers.rationale: Largest US homebuilder; directly hit by higher mortgage rates compressing affordability and order volumes. Article explicitly cites mortgage rate channel.
Same mechanism as DHI — homebuilder demand is rate-sensitive at the margin; a fresh mortgage rate leg higher kills the spring/summer selling season.
Southwest is the most jet-fuel-exposed major airline (least hedged historically, domestic point-to-point); fuel is ~25-30% of opex and passes through with lag.
Jet fuel cost spike compresses margins; international exposure also at risk if Middle East airspace closures force rerouting (longer block hours, higher costs).
Cruise line with high bunker fuel exposure plus discretionary consumer demand hit by squeezed real wages cited in article. Double exposure.
Domino's — squeezed wages + higher gas prices hit low-end consumer discretionary spend and delivery driver economics. Restaurant traffic falls in oil shocks historically.
Zillow's transaction-volume-linked revenue (Premier Agent, mortgages) collapses when mortgage rates spike and existing home sales freeze.
Rocket Companies — mortgage originator with refinance volume nearly zero already; higher rates kill purchase originations too. Pure-play rate-sensitive loser.

headlines: The Iran war could be a $300 billion shock — driving up mortgage rates and squeezing wages

macro_events.event_summary: April 2026 inflation print came in hot, building pressure on Fed Chair Warsh to hike rates rather than cut.
primary_drivers: Higher-for-longer/rising policy rates lifting front-end and real yields
Discount rate increase pressuring long-duration equities
Steeper net interest margin tailwind for asset-sensitive lenders

beneficiaries.rationale: Charles Schwab is highly asset-sensitive; higher short rates reduce cash-sorting drag and lift NIM on sweep balances — a direct earnings tailwind from a hawkish Fed surprise.
Bank of America has the largest asset sensitivity among money-center banks (~$3B+ NII per 100bps parallel shift disclosed); a hike repricing benefits NII directly.
Intercontinental Exchange benefits from rate-volatility driven volumes in interest rate futures and Treasury complex (ICE BofA indices, MOVE-linked activity) when Fed path is in flux.
CME captures spike in SOFR/Fed Funds futures and Treasury futures volume whenever the rate path is repriced hawkishly — direct revenue link to rate uncertainty.
Moody's and rating agencies see fee uplift from refinancing waves and corporate issuance pulled forward ahead of further hikes; less direct but historically correlated.

losers.rationale: D.R. Horton — homebuilders directly hit as 30yr mortgage rates rise with hike expectations, suppressing demand and order pace. Highest beta to rate shocks in the sector.
Lennar same mechanism as DHI; mortgage rate sensitivity translates directly into cancellation rates and incentive spend pressuring margins.
Zillow revenue tied to housing transaction volumes (Premier Agent, mortgage origination via Zillow Home Loans); higher rates freeze the transaction market.
High-multiple growth name (>50x sales) — pure duration trade, gets de-rated mechanically on real yield spikes regardless of fundamentals.
Realty Income — triple-net REIT is a bond proxy with high financial leverage; cap rates expand and refinancing cost rises on a hawkish Fed.

headlines: April’s inflation spike leaves Warsh and the Fed zero excuses not to raise rates
